description: Conditional Access policy requirements from the CIS Microsoft 365 Foundations Benchmark v6.0.1. Level 1 (L1) controls are practical, minimum-security recommendations suitable for all organisations. Level 2 (L2) controls are for higher-security environments and may require additional licensing.

policies.name: Block Legacy Authentication
policies.fullName: CIS L1 1.1 — Ensure Legacy Authentication is blocked via Conditional Access
policies.description: Legacy authentication protocols do not support MFA and can be abused to bypass Conditional Access. A Conditional Access policy SHALL block all legacy authentication clients including Other Clients and Exchange ActiveSync.

policies.name: Require MFA for All Users
policies.fullName: CIS L1 1.2 — Ensure MFA is enabled for all users via Conditional Access
policies.description: Multi-factor authentication must be required for all users accessing Microsoft 365 cloud applications via Conditional Access. This is the foundational identity control for all environments.

policies.name: Require MFA for Admin Roles
policies.fullName: CIS L1 1.3 — Ensure MFA is required for all administrator roles via Conditional Access
policies.description: All users assigned to administrator roles must be required to use MFA via Conditional Access. Privileged accounts are high-value targets and must be protected with strong authentication.

policies.name: Require MFA for Azure Management
policies.fullName: CIS L1 1.4 — Ensure MFA is required for Azure management via Conditional Access
policies.description: MFA must be required for access to Azure management interfaces (Azure Portal, Azure CLI, Azure PowerShell) to prevent unauthorised subscription and resource modifications.

policies.name: Sign-In Risk-Based Conditional Access
policies.fullName: CIS L2 1.5 — Ensure sign-in risk policy is configured via Conditional Access
policies.description: A Conditional Access policy leveraging Entra ID Identity Protection should be configured to require MFA or block access for sign-ins with medium or high sign-in risk levels.

policies.name: User Risk-Based Conditional Access
policies.fullName: CIS L2 1.6 — Ensure user risk policy is configured via Conditional Access
policies.description: A Conditional Access policy leveraging Entra ID Identity Protection should be configured to block access or require password change for users with a high user risk level.

policies.name: Require Managed Device for Sensitive Access
policies.fullName: CIS L2 1.7 — Ensure managed device compliance is required for sensitive application access
policies.description: A Conditional Access policy should require compliant or Entra hybrid joined devices for accessing sensitive cloud applications to ensure device security posture is enforced.
